meta.title: micro

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: micro
root.mentor_note: micro is mega turned around: the same instinct for scale, aimed the other way.

attempt.direction_prompt: Visible to the naked eye, or too small to see?

context_sentences: A single microbe, invisible to the naked eye, had shut down the entire production line.
The lake looked perfectly clear, though a glass of it held millions of microbes.
Before scanners existed, the archive preserved every newspaper on rolls of microfilm.
She spent the afternoon hunched over a reader, scrolling through decades of microfilm for one obituary.
The dispute over one shared kitchen became a microcosm of everything wrong with the entire office.
Critics called the small border town a microcosm of the whole country’s divided politics.
From a thousand feet up, the pilot of the microlight could see the whole valley at once.
Learning to fly a microlight took him barely a fortnight, though landing one calmly took much longer.

construct_options.text: A very small, lightweight aircraft built for one or two people
A tiny handheld torch used by hikers at night
A miniature model of an aircraft, never meant to fly

context_sentences: The valley floor held its own microclimate, ten degrees cooler than the ridge above it.
Gardeners along the sheltered wall had discovered a microclimate warm enough for lemon trees.

construct_options.text: The distinct weather of one small area, different from the region around it
A tiny weather station used to record rainfall
A sudden, violent storm that appears without warning
